verdict_reason: Reverse-translating the engineering layer back to the verse's Sanskrit structure: (1) vihāya kāmān yaḥ sarvān → 'desires abandoned' (component 1); (2) pumāṁś carati niḥspṛhaḥ → 'longing absent' (component 2); (3) nirmamaḥ → 'without mine' (component 3); (4) nirahaṅkāraḥ → 'without I-as-doer' (component 4); (5) sa śāntim adhigacchati → 'Peace, says Krishna, follows'. Verbatim component-by-component preservation. Shankara's specific reading (nirmama as absence of mine-thought even when objects are accepted; nirahaṅkāra as absence of doership-conceit) is operationalised faithfully — the senior author still authors the RFC, still merges the PR, but is not bound by either. Ramanuja's reading (the four-fold disposition as preparation for brāhmī-sthiti's devotion) is preserved at the source-pack level. The falsifiability clause closes the abandonment misread. No distortion. Pass on both 3.6 and 7.10.
